KDEllipsPy
  • API Reference

API Reference

class kdellipspy.config_parser.ConfigParser(filepath: str)[source]

Bases: object

Parses and stores the inversion configuration from the ‘input.ctl’ file. (Parsea y almacena la configuración de la inversión desde el archivo ‘input.ctl’.)

This class is responsible for reading seismic event parameters, station grids, filtering frequencies, and Neighbourhood Algorithm (NA) parameters, structuring them for use throughout the pipeline. (Esta clase es responsable de leer los parámetros del evento sísmico, la grilla de estaciones, las frecuencias de filtrado y los parámetros del algoritmo NA, estructurándolos para su uso en el pipeline.)

filepath

Path to the ‘input.ctl’ control file. (Ruta al archivo de control ‘input.ctl’.)

Type:

str or Path

source_position

Seismic source parameters. (Parámetros de la fuente sísmica.)

Type:

SourceConfig

stations

List and information of stations. (Lista e información de las estaciones.)

Type:

StationConfig

ellipse

Ellipse geometry parameters and frequencies. (Parámetros geométricos de la elipse y frecuencias.)

Type:

EllipseConfig

inversion_process

NA algorithm parameters. (Parámetros del algoritmo de inversión NA.)

Type:

InversionConfig

parse() → None[source]

Parse the entire control file

to_numpy() → ndarray[source]

Exporta las capas directamente como una matriz de NumPy (Nx6). Este es exactamente el formato que requiere el wrapper de Axitra.

kdellipspy.config_parser.parse_velocity_model(filepath: str) → List[Dict[str, float]][source]

Compatibility helper for existing code path.

kdellipspy.config_parser.read_input_ctl(filepath: str) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Compatibility helper returning a flat dictionary used by the pipeline.

kdellipspy.config_parser.validate_input_ctl(filepath: str) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Return a concise parse summary for smoke testing.

kdellipspy.signal_utils.bandpass_filter_waveforms(synthetic: ndarray, time: ndarray, freq1: float, freq2: float, corners: int = 4, zerophase: bool = True) → ndarray[source]

Apply Butterworth bandpass filter to synthetic waveforms.

This ensures synthetic waveforms match the frequency band of observed data, maintaining consistency in the misfit calculation.

Parameters:

  • synthetic – waveform array with shape (n_stations, 3, npts)

  • time – time array with shape (npts,)

  • freq1 – low frequency cutoff (Hz)

  • freq2 – high frequency cutoff (Hz)

  • corners – filter order (default 4 = 4th order)

  • zerophase – if True, apply filter forward-backward for zero phase distortion

Returns:

filtered waveforms with same shape as input

Return type:

filtered_synthetic

Raises:

ValueError – if time array has < 2 points or freq2 >= Nyquist frequency

kdellipspy.signal_utils.build_azi_times_array(input_ctl_path: str | Path = 'input.ctl', model_name: str = 'iasp91', p_shift_s: float = -1.0, s_shift_s: float = -1.0, use_iris: bool = False) → ndarray[source]

Build legacy-compatible azi_times values as an array with columns: [azimuth_rad, tP, tS].

This reproduces the old script behavior while avoiding IRIS network requirements by default (use_iris=False).

kdellipspy.signal_utils.load_and_filter_observed_data(freq1: float | None = None, freq2: float | None = None, input_ctl_path: str | Path = 'input.ctl', data_dir: str | Path = 'DATA', prefer_raw: bool = False) → Tuple[ndarray, ndarray][source]

Load observed data with two supported user workflows:

  1. Flat files in DATA: - real_disp_x/y/z (if input.ctl units=1) - real_vel_x/y/z (if input.ctl units=2) Each file must contain 2 columns: time and data, and total rows must be npts * nstations.

  2. RAW files in DATA/RAW: - .sac or .mseed files - station name extracted from waveform metadata - bandpass filtering using input.ctl frequency band - response removal attempted when inventory files are present in RAW

Returns:

ndarray with shape (n_stations, 3, npts) time_array: ndarray with shape (npts,)

Return type:

observed_waveforms

kdellipspy.signal_utils.write_azi_times_file(input_ctl_path: str | Path = 'input.ctl', output_path: str | Path | None = None, model_name: str = 'iasp91', p_shift_s: float = -1.0, s_shift_s: float = -1.0, use_iris: bool = False) → Path[source]

Write Event/azi_times.txt with legacy-compatible 3-column format.
